channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Johnnette Technologies to Unveil New Defence Systems at North Tech Symposium 2026, VTOL Drones Emerge as Key Highlight
item.description: Johnnette Technologies is set to showcase a new suite of defence technologies at the North Tech Symposium 2026, positioning the event as a platform for unveiling next-generation capabilities aimed at enhancing precision, performance, and operational effectiveness. The exhibition will take place from May 4 to 6 at the COBRA Auditorium in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh, where the company will be presenting its systems at Stall B-53.
item.dc:creator: IDRW.ORG

item.title: Beyond Blue Water: Indian Navy Orders 14,000+ Jungle Boots for Littoral and Special Operations Readiness
item.description: The Indian Navy’s procurement of 14,274 pairs of Jungle Boots may appear routine at first glance, but the scale and specifications point to a deliberate capability refresh for units operating far from traditional “blue water” roles. This acquisition reflects the Navy’s expanding operational footprint across coastal, riverine, and tropical environments, where ground mobility, endurance, and survivability are as critical as maritime capability.
item.dc:creator: IDRW.ORG

item.title: India Waits Europe’s responses in its interest to join 6th gen jet program
item.description: In a significant development for India’s defence modernisation, Defence Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh has announced that the country is actively exploring collaboration opportunities for a sixth-generation fighter aircraft program. Highlighting the enormous investment required for such advanced technology, Singh emphasised that any progress in this domain would necessitate strong international partnerships.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: LCA Tejas Mk2 Gets Boost as CSIR-NAL Delivers First Composite Centre Fuselage Sections for Second Prototype
item.description: India’s Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft pipeline continues to gather momentum as CSIR–National Aerospace Laboratories (NAL) has handed over the first set of composite components for the centre fuselage of the LCA Air Force Mk2 to the Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) and Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL). The delivery marks a critical milestone in the transition from design to fabrication for the Mk2 program and reflects a deliberate push to deepen indigenous capability in complex aerostructures.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: DRDO Chief Confirms 13-Ton Ghatak UCAV Will Be Tejas-Class Stealth Jet Powered by Kaveri Derivative Engine
item.description: India’s indigenous unmanned combat air vehicle program has taken a significant step forward, with DRDO Chairman Dr. Samir V. Kamat confirming key design parameters of the Ghatak UCAV. The platform is being developed as a 13-ton class stealth combat system, broadly comparable in size and weight to the LCA Tejas, but optimized for fully autonomous strike missions in contested environments.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Can develop Agni-VI Missile if Government gives Nod : DRDO Chief
item.description: India’s next-generation long-range ballistic missile program, Agni-VI, remains contingent on a formal government decision, with DRDO Chairman Dr. Samir V. Kamat confirming that the organisation is technically prepared to move forward whenever approval is granted. His remarks indicate that the developmental groundwork within DRDO has reached a level of maturity where the program can be initiated without significant lead time once sanctioned.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: DRDO Hypersonic Glide Missile Nears First Trials, Glide Phase Tech Ahead of Cruise Variant: Chairman Samir V Kamat
item.description: India’s push into hypersonic weapons is entering a decisive phase, with DRDO Chairman Dr. Samir V Kamat indicating that the country’s hypersonic glide missile could undergo its first trials in the near term. According to Kamat, the glide missile program is currently ahead of the hypersonic cruise missile effort, with testing expected “fairly soon,” signaling tangible progress in one of the most complex areas of modern missile technology.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Brahmos Aerospace confirms getting two export orders in 2025-26
item.description: Brahmos Aerospace is meeting all the requirements of armed forces within stipulated timelines.
item.dc:creator: Company Press Release

item.title: MKU becomes first Indian private defence company to secure contracts for life extension and overhaul of IAF aircraft fleet
item.description: This recognition from the Indian Air Force reflects a significant shift in how fleet sustainment responsibilities are being distributed, and underscores the growing confidence in MKU’s engineering capabilities, quality standards, and capacity to deliver mission-critical outcomes.
item.dc:creator: Company Press Release

item.title: MDL today delivered the Fourth Stealth Frigate of the P17A Class, Mahendragiri to the Indian Navy.
item.description: The ship is indigenously built, showcasing cutting-edge technology and a strong commitment to the Government of India’s “#MakeinIndia” initiative.
item.dc:creator: Company Press Release

item.title: Op Sindoor demonstrated a shift from old mindset of issuing diplomatic statements on terror attacks, it showcased PM Modi-led Govt’s unwavering commitment through decisive action: Raksha Mantri
item.description: “Terrorism emanates from a distorted and perverse mindset. It casts a dark stain on humanity. The battle against terrorism is not merely a matter of national security; it is, fundamentally, a battle to safeguard the core values ??of humanity. It is a fight against a barbaric ideology that stands in direct opposition to every human value. We have articulated this Indian perspective, both within the country and abroad,” said Shri Rajnath Singh.
item.dc:creator: PIB

item.title: Nepal Airlines apologises for showing J-K as part of Pakistan in ‘network map’
item.description: The airlines had deleted the social media post on Wednesday following the backlash. “We sincerely apologise for error in the network map recently shared on our social media channels. The map contained significant cartographic inaccuracies regarding international boundaries that do not reflect the official stance of Nepal or Nepal Airlines,” it posted on X.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: Raksha Mantri to hold bilateral talks with his Italian counterpart in New Delhi
item.description: Defence cooperation between India and Italy gained further momentum after Raksha Mantri visited Rome in October 2023. Defence Minister Crosetto’s maiden visit to India underlines the desire of both nations to further expand existing cooperation and explore new areas of collaboration particularly under the industrial partnership domain.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: CORAS commandos among 21 security personnel to guard Vande Bharat trains in J-K: PCSC
item.description: Vaishnaw on Thursday flagged off the 20-coach train, which is expected to cut travel time between Jammu and Srinagar to five hours, here in the presence of Chief Minister Omar Abdullah and Union Minister Dr Jitendra Singh.
item.dc:creator: PTI

item.title: BRO Eastern Region Chief Engineers’ Meet Charts Road Map To Accelerate Strategic Infra Development
item.description: Defence spokesperson Lieutenant Colonel Mahendra Rawat stated that the conference took place on April 28 and 29 under the chairmanship of Jitendra Prasad, Additional Director General Border Roads (East).
item.dc:creator: IANS
